title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · Changed-Data Lab
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an audited service-metrics query pack with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.
Set result grain with GROUP BY and verify that every selected value is grouped or aggregated.
Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Aggregate Functions
steps.instruction:
lab case — Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 4 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Aggregate Functions.

FROM tickets
GROUP BY team_id
ORDER BY team_id;
